item.title: Keep up the good fight.
item.description:
Illinois Gouvernor J. B. Pritzker, in a commencement speech at Northwestern University in Evanston, Illinois in June 2023 Cruelty and prejudice are inbuilt human traits. Empathy and compassion are evolved states of being and require the mental capacity to step past our most primal urges.
Moms for Liberty see social emotional learning as a stepping stone for Marxist indoctrination of kids. https://portal.momsforliberty.org/news/epoch-times-m4l-fights-remove-sel-from-classrooms/

item.title: Cute art information
item.description:
source: https://bsky.app/profile/fablefulart.bsky.social/post/3lgjfibexvk23
DO NOT TAKE YOUR POCKET SIZED TRACKING DEVICE. Also - do not use biometrics, police can unlock with those. Do not willfully unlock as that proves ownership.

item.title: Aunt Cass - Big Hero 6 Cosplay
item.description:
My Aunt Cass Cosplay! It's nice finally being able to use my real hair for a cosplay instead of wearing a wig for this one... That has only ever happened... Like once before in the last decade? I'm talking about wigs so you can pretend you are looking at this post for reasons outside of everyone's crush on Aunt Cass...
Don't pretend like you haven't seen this reference image before.
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.dc:creator: Joxtar

item.title: I once got bit by a man who later killed my co-worker.
item.description:
I was working as a direct support professional in a home that I believe did not have proper safety measures in place. I was supporting two violent adult men and was left alone in the house for hours at a time. One of the men was known for trying to sexually assault female staff. The other was known for physically assaulting staff. This all happened because he didn’t have the snacks he wanted. First I was cornered in the hallway, then hit in the face, and while trying to block my face he saw the opportunity and bit down hard. Thank god I was wearing a light jacket, otherwise I think I might have a chunk missing from my arm. He didn’t release for a good 30 seconds, as I was pleading for him to let go. After he let go I ran to the office and locked myself in. I called the manager on-call and said I needed to be relieved so I could go to the hospital. At first they tried to tell me no one could relieve me but eventually a VERY pregnant woman showed up. I hope she was safe. I don’t know, I never went back.
At the hospital getting my shots.
The muscle was bruised and sore for 6 months after the bite.
When I first started, an older lady was training me and showed me all of the bite/scratch scars she had acquired over the many years of working there. She warned that I would get bit eventually. Not an “if” but “when”. We are trained for this kind of attack, I thought I could handle myself. I couldn’t believe this poor woman, who loved the men she cared for, still wanted to stick around. Apparently her family begged her to leave the job, but she refused. A couple years later, I see an article that she was found in the bathroom covered in blood and with a broken neck. She died a few days later in the hospital. The family is trying to sue, I haven’t really seen many updates about it. The worst part? He didn’t face any criminal charges and still lives in the same house. I’ve been through a lot with the people I’ve supported over the last decade, but I’ve never ever resented any of them before. I hate this man. Here’s an article of the incident. ETA a better news article: https://www.bluemountaineagle.com/news/the-death-of-bobbie-kolada-part-1-did-somebody-do-this-to-her/article_e7e1ce36-e470-536d-8f41-2fe250edd591.html This is a 5 part series that discusses the journalistic investigation that was completed about the attack on my Co-worker.
item.dc:creator: LaFlamme4182

item.description: EDIT: dang, this is the fastest I’ve reached the front page in awhile. Thanks for the upvotes gang. I’ve had a really awful day (who hasn’t though, this particular January), and the fake internet points helped. Feel free to send LEGO stuff, boobs, and your favorite pasta recipes.

item.title: When you finally get to play the game
item.dc:creator: edwardbear786

item.title: New love in my life
item.description: This is her! Adopted her from a local rescue group. Edit: I haven't named her yet. I'm leaning toward calling her Megami, but I'm open to suggestions! It'll be a day or two before I get a tag and collar for her. Further edit: Haha, "Mega" was actually what the rescue group was calling her. I think a child of one of the volunteers wanted to call her Megatron, and the group just started calling her Mega. Megami is the Japanese word for goddess. I was thinking of anime names because she has big anime eyes. Keeping a bit of her original name, combined with my thoughts, and that's how I got to Megami.
item.dc:creator: TheBizi

item.title: Now even the cake is cake
item.dc:creator: Razade

item.title: Stop Kidnapping kittens.
item.description: So, I understand if you're rescuing an entire litter +mom, or found an abandoned kitten, but please stop taking kittens without giving it some thought. Look for other kittens, look for mom, call some experts. And sure as hell don't hand a tiny kitten to a toddler.
item.dc:creator: DrYuaTakeda

item.title: Bill Murray is a legend
item.description: Imgur, I've been going through a hard time for a while. After having moved to Estonia with my ex wife (her homeland) to start a new life, I've been on the streets and unable to secure a job. I try to land any service industry job as well as construction, but I've had zero luck. I want to return to Belgium (where I'm from) and start to get back on my feet there. I'm a hard worker and have just not been given the chance I need over here. I understand everyone has issues and times are tough, but IF you can help, please let me know and I can send my Paypal link. If not, then I don't blame you. Happy scrolling x You guys are all amazing and this community has been my happy place during my dark times.
item.dc:creator: Skeebeedeebop

item.title: Streaming services literally have micro transactions now. I had to buy a 5.99 add-on to prime to watch a critically acclaimed show that no one even watches because it’s on an obscure platform no one’s going to pay for

item.dc:creator: AggressiveProgressive

item.title: Acts of resistance that AREN'T protesting or voting
item.description:
1
2
3
4
5
6
Many people ask me what you can do to resist and be active and supportive that doesn't specifically include protesting and voting. So, with permission, I'm sharing this document that is maintained by someone else online. https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OSWxykA1WHOi0vTPLAJDaCeVhR3uSfh7PhlCj4t4yT0/edit?usp=sharing The links are available on the document itself, so please leverage this. This is by no means a complete list either. There are millions of ways you can support. Get creative and stand up! Edit: I want to share that an amazing poc trans person made and maintains this list. They have asked to be anonymous on other social media sites, so I will not be sharing their name or handle here. Edit 2: Adding an additional link to resources such as the simple sabotage manual: https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/26184?ref=404media.co
item.dc:creator: chaosbuni
